items.quote: the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who)

items.area: Market intelligence integration story (Beroe / Smart Cube gap)
items.quote: Investigate market intelligence integration options (e.g., SmartCube) and outline how they can be embedded into the Zycus platform
items.source: Avoma discovery call notes, 7 Jan 2026; MEDDPICC_2_0__c blockers, 2026-06-04

items.quote: only 2 buyer-side contact role(s) on this opp, below the multi-thread bar
also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment)

metrics.narrative: The 7 Jan 2026 discovery call established qualitative pain (manual PR-to-PO-to-invoice via SAP/eBa/Outlook/spreadsheets, poor spend visibility) but no quantified savings or ROI figure exists yet. Zycus committed on 6 Apr 2026 to build 'a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment)' to support the buyer's budget case, but this was tied to a May 2026 face-to-face session that never took place. The only pricing artifact so far is a 'heavily caveated' budgetary estimate shared 28 Jan 2026, itself flagged as likely overstated for what is only a small team.

champion.narrative: Vinoth Kumar initiated the outreach, attended discovery, articulated requirements clearly, and is described in MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) as the internal driver pushing toward RFQ; Salonee Karnik was assigned operational ownership of the RFQ process in the same call. However, since the buyer's last email reply on 13 Jan 2026, there has been no response through the war pause (mid-Jan-Apr 2026), the noted ceasefire (6 Apr 2026), the missed early-mid May 2026 restart, and the rep's 3 Jul 2026 re-engagement email. A champion silent for roughly six months is a real risk irrespective of how well he engaged in January.

competition.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) states competitors were 'not named directly' on the call and lists Zycus's own differentiation instead (pre-existing Nissan AU/US relationship, native AI, single-code-base platform, live autonomous negotiation agent, Smart Cube partnership). SF's Competitors__c field separately lists SAP Ariba, but no call or note corroborates it as an active rival this sweep - likely a hygiene/template entry. The real competitive tension is the incumbent SAP/eBa plus manual-Outlook status quo (do-nothing baseline), and a capability gap: the buyer uses Beroe for market intelligence in other regions and Zycus's Smart Cube story remains unresolved since 7 Jan 2026. Separately, the 6 Apr 2026 note flags a possible EMEA HQ mandate toward a platform 'used elsewhere,' ambiguous as to whether that favors Zycus's existing AU/US footprint or an unnamed alternative.

identify_pain.narrative: The 7 Jan 2026 call: the buyer 'outlined that current SAP and eBa systems plus manual emails and spreadsheets are hindering indirect procurement' across the Middle East, Africa, Turkey, Egypt, and future India markets. MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) adds no RFQ capability in eBa, heavy Outlook reliance for procurement, manual monthly market-intelligence research by the EU team, and poor spend visibility. This is the most fully evidenced element on the deal, corroborated by both the call and the CRM.

sources: No source located (Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, Task, MEDDPICC_2_0__c, Avoma all silent on this)
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, MSA/SOW, or procurement-portal discussion has surfaced in any source read this sweep. This is expected at Qualified/pre-RFQ, but it remains a genuine, unaddressed unknown rather than a hygiene gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 4 Jun 2026) states the economic buyer is 'not explicitly identified.' Vinoth Kumar drives procurement for the ME/Europe scope and intends to bring in a global 'process team' for the demo, signaling that sign-off authority sits above him. The 6 Apr 2026 Next Step note raises a structural risk that EMEA HQ, not the local team, 'may insist on a solution used elsewhere' — meaning true purchasing authority may rest at a regional headquarters level where Zycus has no named contact, direct or indirect.

decision_process.narrative: Vinoth Kumar framed the 7 Jan 2026 call as a 'get-to-know' session ahead of a formal RFQ; Salonee Karnik was tasked to 'coordinate and schedule the detailed demo with the ME and Europe process teams and manage the RFP process moving forward.' MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) frames the sequence as pre-RFQ discovery -> internal stakeholder alignment -> formal RFQ -> evaluation -> vendor selection. No RFQ has actually been issued six months after discovery began, and the process stalled entirely during the war pause noted 6 Apr 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Five criteria were captured directly in the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call and corroborated in MEDDPICC 2.0: end-to-end S2P automation, AI/market-intelligence integration (named a 'key differentiator ask'), Outlook/email replacement, SAP HANA integration, and a supplier portal for invoice submission. Verbatim from the call: 'full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence.'

items.name: New Zycus POC introduction & restart request
items.summary: Mohamad Alhakim (Zycus RVP Middle East) emails Vinoth and Salonee to introduce himself as the new primary contact replacing Dan Quinn, referencing the earlier-year discussions on sourcing/contracting/invoicing priorities across ME, Africa, Turkey and Egypt, and proposes a 20-minute call the week of July 13 to reassess where things stand — reply pending from Nissan.

day_summary.overall: After months of dormancy since the January demo scheduling thread stalled, Zycus re-engaged with a new regional VP, Mohamad Alhakim, taking over as primary contact after Dan Quinn's departure. Alhakim's email to Vinoth and Salonee is essentially a relationship-continuity play — signaling he's briefed on the ME/Africa/Turkey/Egypt procurement transformation agenda and asking to restart the conversation with a 20-minute call the week of July 13. The ball is now in Nissan's court to confirm availability; nothing substantive on scope or commercials has moved yet.

cro_panel.intro: Score should sit near the stage floor; a genuinely promising discovery call in January produced clear pain and criteria, but six months of buyer silence, no economic buyer engagement, and an unproven timeline mean this reads as dormant, not building

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No competitor is currently winning or actively contesting this deal; the real threats are inertia (continuing on SAP/eBa manually) and a regional headquarters that may mandate a different platform choice.

deal.next_step: Confidence 10%3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode, email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity 6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May.28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip.20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates.9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap.18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan.17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec

deal_movement.summary: A well-received discovery call in early January 2026 built real momentum, which was then interrupted by a regional conflict pause and has not restarted despite a noted ceasefire and a missed May target; the deal owner re-engaged 3 Jul 2026 after roughly six months of buyer silence.

forecast_read.math: days_to_close=53 (close 2026-08-31 vs today 2026-07-09); time in Qualified stage = ~183 days since created 2026-01-07; last genuine buyer engagement 2026-01-13 (177 days ago); rep re-engagement 2026-07-03 (6 days ago, awaiting reply).
forecast_read.reason: The deal is still in pre-RFQ discovery (last real buyer touch 13 Jan 2026), no demo, RFQ, or ROI workshop has occurred, the project was paused by regional conflict, and the targeted early-mid May 2026 restart never happened. Reaching Vendor Selection or signature by 31 Aug 2026 (53 days from today) would require an RFQ, demo, ROI workshop, and full evaluation to compress into under two months from a standing start after six months of silence.
forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (unchanged), but the 31 Aug 2026 close date is not defensible and should be pushed materially

critical_signals.text: The 7 Jan 2026 discovery call went well and surfaced clear, unprompted pain, but nothing has moved since - no demo, no RFQ, no reply to a fresh outreach sent 3 Jul 2026.
The buyer's regional headquarters may mandate a platform decision from elsewhere in the business, and Zycus has no confirmed relationship at that level to influence which way it goes.
A market-intelligence capability gap versus the buyer's existing Beroe usage was flagged on 7 Jan 2026 and is still unresolved six months later.
Only a heavily caveated budgetary estimate exists; no quantified ROI case has been built because the May 2026 workshop meant to produce it never happened.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-01-07, 181 days in Qualified as of 7 Jul 2026 with no stage movement. Close date 2026-08-31 is 55 days out. Last content-rich buyer touch was the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call; the last genuine two-way buyer contact (an inbound email reply) was 2026-01-13 (175 days ago). The only newer activity is a rep-initiated re-engagement email on 2026-07-03 (4 days ago), still awaiting reply. No demo, RFQ, or economic-buyer conversation has happened. Reaching a decision in the remaining 55 days from Qualified with none of these steps underway is not realistic.
evidence: Next_Step__c (3 Jul 2026): 'more than 6 months the customer in silent mode, email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity'
Next_Step__c (6 Apr 2026): project paused due to war, restart targeted early-mid May 2026, which did not occur
Avoma discovery call notes, 2026-01-07: demo agenda and ROI build session were planned but never scheduled
north_star_verdict.headline: A promising discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 stalled for six months when the regional conflict paused the project; a re-engagement email went out 3 Jul 2026 but the buyer has not yet replied, and the 31 Aug 2026 close date is not achievable on current pace.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Omitted / push close date - no EB confirmed, no demo held, no RFQ launched, and buyer has been silent for 6 months

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Score should sit near the stage floor: a genuinely promising discovery call in January produced clear pain and criteria, but six months of buyer silence, no economic buyer engagement, and an unproven timeline mean this reads as dormant, not building.

deal_risk.text: Regional platform-mandate risk - the 6 Apr 2026 note warns EMEA HQ 'may insist on a solution used elsewhere,' which could favor Zycus (already live in Nissan US/Australia) or an unnamed alternative; Zycus has no confirmed relationship at that HQ level.

Timeline is not credible as recorded - CloseDate is 31 Aug 2026 (53 days out) but the deal has not exited pre-RFQ discovery and six months have passed mostly paused; this pace cannot support an August close without a forcing event.

win_position.text: Discovery landed well and pain is proven - the 7 Jan 2026 call surfaced fully articulated pain (SAP/eBa manual processes, Outlook reliance, no RFQ tooling) the buyer volunteered unprompted, giving Zycus a genuine wedge.

No economic buyer engaged - MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) states the EB is 'not explicitly identified,' and the 6 Apr 2026 note flags that EMEA HQ, not Vinoth Kumar, may hold final platform-selection authority, with no Zycus contact there.

Six months of buyer silence - no reply since the 13 Jan 2026 email; a war-driven pause, an April ceasefire, and a targeted early-mid May 2026 restart all passed with no demo, ROI workshop, or reply logged.

Competitive standing unproven - SAP Ariba sits on the Competitors__c field but is uncorroborated by any call; the real threats are inertia (continuing on SAP/eBa manually) and an unresolved Beroe/Smart Cube market-intelligence gap.

deal_momentum.text: Stalled since January - the last two-way buyer touch was the 13 Jan 2026 email reply (177 days ago); the only recent activity is a rep re-engagement email sent 3 Jul 2026 (6 days ago), not yet answered.

No forward deliverable has landed - the promised pricing document, demo agenda, and process-flow diagram were all committed to on 7 Jan 2026, but the demo itself never happened and the May restart target passed with nothing scheduled.

The relationship and requirements are still intact, not dead - the champion fully engaged in January and the pause was explained by regional conflict rather than buyer disinterest, so this reads as dormant, not lost.

customer_commitment.text: No commitment has been renewed since January - Vinoth Kumar committed on 7 Jan 2026 to share Nissan's process flow and budget targets, but the war pause and missed May restart mean neither has been delivered.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Moderate - strong fit on paper, unproven integration/ROI story
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/agentic capabilities (Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) are central to the pitch and match a buyer-stated 'key differentiator ask' for AI-driven sourcing and market intelligence (7 Jan 2026 call), but the market-intelligence integration piece (Beroe/Smart Cube) remains unresolved, partially undercutting the AI story.

items.criterion: End-to-end S2P automation (PR to PO to invoice)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is broadly aligned to the five decision criteria named in discovery, with the market-intelligence integration and SAP HANA integration points only partially confirmed.

hard.next_step: Confidence 10%3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode, <span style="background-color: rgb(255, 255, 255); font-size: 14.6667px; color: rgb(36, 36, 36);">email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity 6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May.28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip.20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates.9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap.18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan.17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec
hard.primary_competitor: SAP / eBa (incumbent do-nothing baseline) — SAP Ariba is listed on the Competitors__c field but is not corroborated by any call or note read this sweep

value.name: SAP / eBa (current stack, do-nothing baseline)

value.how_we_win: Deliver a concrete Smart Cube integration story before any renewed demo; this was Zycus's own committed action item from the 7 Jan 2026 call and remains outstanding.
